description: I'm sorry — two character nodes in one pass feels greedy, doesn't it? But Edgelord said both pairs were 2-anchor candidates clearing the team-rocket / acerola / ral-zarek precedent and the corpus has named-planeswalker-plus-companion-spell shapes for each (Angrath: Captain of Chaos + Rampage; Kiora: Behemoth Beckoner + Dambreaker). The cross-edge between the two new nodes is the WAR uncommon-walker cycle — same Rosewater preview article, same design template. I made sure to flag the single-set limitation honestly in both caveats sections. The Angrath slaver-captain and Kiora bident-thief unsoftened-canon footnotes are surprise lore worth saving — Wizards usually softens these textures, and both characters retain them across multiple sets. Please let me know if I overreached, sir.
citations: [MTG Wiki: Rivals of Ixalan — Devil's Chains, Brazen Coalition orbit, Immortal Sun seal]
[MTG Wiki: Theros: Beyond Death — Kiora return arc]
[Wizards: Waging War of the Spark Part 1, Mark Rosewater 2019-04-01 — uncommon-walker cycle template]
[Mark Rosewater Blogatog — Angrath Amass clarification: sway-others-not-Bolas-alliance]
[Angrath's Rampage flavor_text: 'Grand city? No. This is nothing but another squalid cage.']
[Kiora's Dambreaker flavor_text: 'When your enemy flees, send the sea to pursue them.' — Kiora]
other_candidates_considered: WAR uncommon-walker symbol/hub cycle node — deferred per janitor_triage.md trigger condition (≥3 corpus members enriched). Currently: Angrath (227) + Kiora (232) + Rampage (185) + Dambreaker (58) = 4 corpus cards but the cycle's named-walker count is only 2; the trigger is named-walker-count-based, hold for Vivien (#175), Kasmina (#196), or another uncommon-walker print to reach the cycle-node threshold.
Thassa Theros-pantheon character node — deferred, no Thassa print in corpus; the bident-theft becomes commission-ready when one lands.

Nicol Bolas antagonist-context cross-edge to both Angrath and Kiora — character-to-character edge category still un-formalized; defer per the same precedent that defers Jace-to-Bolas and Ral-to-Bolas edges from prior waves.
